title: pittosporum pervillei
intro: Pittosporum pervillei, also known as the Raiatea or Toi, is a plant native to the island of Raiatea in French Polynesia, belonging to the Pittosporaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Pittosporum pervillei include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory problems, among others. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as an infusion or a decoction, and it can also be used to make herbal teas, salves, and essential oils.
